sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Are two groups necessarily isomorphic if each of them can be defined by a single relation and is a homomorphic image of the other one? { D.\,I.\,Moldavanski } No, not necessarily (A.\,V.\,Borshchev, D.\,I.\,Moldavanski , Math. Notes, 79, no.\,1 (2006), 31--40). %Mat. Zametki 79, No. 1, 34-44 (2006).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
